text: Which of these characters is a Jedi and the hero of the original 'Star Wars' trilogy?
explanation: Luke Skywalker is the central hero of the original 'Star Wars' trilogy.

text: Which studio created the film 'Toy Story'?
explanation: 'Toy Story' was the first feature film by Pixar.

text: What is the name of the archaeologist with a hat and whip in the adventure film series?
explanation: Indiana Jones is the hero of the adventure films by Spielberg and Lucas.

text: Who played Jack in the film 'Titanic' (1997)?
explanation: Jack Dawson was played by Leonardo DiCaprio.

text: Which classic comedy series features the bumbling spy chief Inspector Clouseau?
explanation: Inspector Clouseau is the hero of 'The Pink Panther' comedies.

text: Who is the main hero of the film series about a young wizard at Hogwarts?
explanation: Harry Potter studies at the school of magic, Hogwarts.

text: What colour is the talking Donkey in the film 'Shrek'?
explanation: Donkey, Shrek's companion, is grey.

text: What is the name of the famous spy known as 'agent 007'?
explanation: Agent 007 is James Bond.

text: Which animated film tells the story of a lion cub named Simba?
explanation: Simba is the hero of 'The Lion King'.

text: In which film does a liner hit an iceberg and sink?
explanation: The sinking of the liner is shown in 'Titanic'.

text: Who created and directed the original 'Star Wars' trilogy?
explanation: 'Star Wars' was created and directed by George Lucas.

text: What is the name of the billionaire superhero in an iron suit from the Marvel universe?
explanation: Tony Stark in his armour is Iron Man.

text: Which Christmas film is famous for the line 'You'll shoot your eye out'?
explanation: That line is from 'A Christmas Story'.

text: What is the name of the clownfish whose father searches for him in the Pixar film?
explanation: This is the film 'Finding Nemo'.

text: In which film are dinosaurs recreated in an island theme park?
explanation: The dinosaur park is shown in 'Jurassic Park'.

text: Who played the boxer Rocky Balboa?
explanation: Rocky was played by Sylvester Stallone, who also wrote the script.

text: Which Marvel superhero climbs walls and shoots webs?
explanation: Web-shooting and wall-crawling are Spider-Man's trademarks.

text: What is the school of magic in the Harry Potter films called?
explanation: Harry Potter studies at Hogwarts.

text: Which green Marvel giant grows stronger the angrier he gets?
explanation: The Hulk gains enormous strength when enraged.

text: Which film features the line 'Why so serious?' spoken by the Joker?
explanation: That line is spoken by Heath Ledger's Joker in 'The Dark Knight'.

text: Who directed the films 'Titanic' and 'Avatar'?
explanation: Both films were directed by James Cameron.

text: Which Christopher Nolan film is about entering people's dreams?
explanation: Travelling through dreams is the subject of 'Inception'.

text: Who played the Joker in the film 'The Dark Knight' (2008)?
explanation: Heath Ledger won a posthumous Oscar for the role of the Joker.

text: Which animated film features a snowman named Olaf?
explanation: Olaf is the friendly snowman in 'Frozen'.

text: Who played Neo in the film 'The Matrix'?
explanation: Neo was played by Keanu Reeves.

text: Who directed 'Pulp Fiction' and 'Kill Bill'?
explanation: Both films were directed by Quentin Tarantino.

text: In which 2012 film did the Marvel team first unite against Loki?
explanation: The team first assembled in 'The Avengers' (2012).

text: Who directed 'Jaws', 'Jurassic Park' and 'Schindler's List'?
explanation: All three films were directed by Steven Spielberg.

text: Which animation studio made 'Shrek' and 'Madagascar'?
explanation: 'Shrek' and 'Madagascar' were made by DreamWorks Animation.

text: Who played the title role in the film 'Forrest Gump'?
explanation: Forrest Gump was played by Tom Hanks.

text: Which Italian film won the Oscar for Best Foreign Language Film and is set in a concentration camp?
explanation: 'Life Is Beautiful' by Roberto Benigni won the Oscar in 1999.

text: Who directed 'Gladiator' and 'Alien'?
explanation: 'Gladiator' and 'Alien' were directed by Ridley Scott.

text: In which saga does Anakin Skywalker become Darth Vader?
explanation: Anakin's turn into Vader is shown in 'Star Wars'.

text: Who played Captain Jack Sparrow in 'Pirates of the Caribbean'?
explanation: Jack Sparrow was played by Johnny Depp.

text: Which studio made the anime 'Spirited Away'?
explanation: 'Spirited Away' was made by Studio Ghibli.

text: Who directed the 'Lord of the Rings' trilogy?
explanation: The trilogy was directed by Peter Jackson.

text: Who played Harry Potter in the film adaptations?
explanation: Harry Potter was played by Daniel Radcliffe.

text: In which Disney film does Princess Elsa have ice powers?
explanation: Elsa is the heroine of 'Frozen'.

text: Who directed the science-fiction films 'Stalker' and 'Solaris'?
explanation: 'Stalker' and 'Solaris' were directed by Andrei Tarkovsky.

text: In which film does Tom Hanks's character survive on a desert island with a ball named 'Wilson'?
explanation: The Wilson ball story is from 'Cast Away'.

text: Which Francis Ford Coppola film is about the Corleone crime family?
options: Once Upon a Time in America
explanation: The Corleone family is at the centre of 'The Godfather'.

text: Who played Vito Corleone in the first 'Godfather' film?
explanation: Don Vito Corleone was played by Marlon Brando.

text: Which silent Eisenstein film is famous for the Odessa Steps sequence?
explanation: The Odessa Steps scene is from 'Battleship Potemkin'.

text: Who directed 'Fight Club' and 'Se7en'?
explanation: Both films were directed by David Fincher.

text: Which South Korean film won the Oscar for Best Picture in 2020?
explanation: 'Parasite' became the first non-English film to win Best Picture.

text: Who directed the film 'Parasite'?
explanation: 'Parasite' was directed by Bong Joon-ho.

text: Which actor played the Terminator in the franchise of the same name?
explanation: The Terminator was played by Arnold Schwarzenegger.

text: Who directed 'Interstellar' and 'Dunkirk'?
explanation: Both films were directed by Christopher Nolan.

text: Which Stanley Kubrick science-fiction film was released in 1968?
explanation: In 1968 Kubrick directed '2001: A Space Odyssey'.

text: Who directed the anime film 'Your Name' (2016)?
explanation: 'Your Name' was directed by Makoto Shinkai.

text: Which Soviet director made the epic 'War and Peace' that won an Oscar?
explanation: 'War and Peace' was directed by Sergei Bondarchuk and won the Oscar in 1969.

text: Who played Travis Bickle in Martin Scorsese's 'Taxi Driver'?
explanation: Travis Bickle was played by Robert De Niro.

text: Which 1982 Ridley Scott film became a cyberpunk classic?
explanation: The 1982 cyberpunk classic is 'Blade Runner'.

text: In which year was the first 'Star Wars' film released?
explanation: The first 'Star Wars' film was released in 1977.

text: Which Mexican director made 'The Shape of Water' and 'Pan's Labyrinth'?
explanation: Both films were directed by Guillermo del Toro.

text: Which Alfred Hitchcock thriller is famous for its shower-murder scene?
explanation: The famous shower scene is from 'Psycho'.

text: Which film features a sled named 'Rosebud' and the press tycoon Charles Foster Kane?
explanation: 'Rosebud' and Kane are from Orson Welles's 'Citizen Kane'.

text: Who directed the South Korean revenge thriller 'Oldboy' (2003)?
explanation: 'Oldboy' was directed by Park Chan-wook.

text: Which 1994 film with Uma Thurman and John Travolta made Tarantino famous?
explanation: It is 'Pulp Fiction', winner of the Palme d'Or.

text: Who directed the thriller 'The Silence of the Lambs' (1991)?
explanation: 'The Silence of the Lambs' was directed by Jonathan Demme and won five major Oscars.

text: Which of these movies is NOT considered to be part of the Marvel Cinematic Universe?
explanation: The correct answer is Fantastic Four (2015).

text: Which of the following is not the name of a "Bond Girl"?
explanation: The correct answer is Vanessa Kensington.

text: Which DC superhero was NOT included in the 2017 movie "Justice League"?
explanation: The correct answer is Green Arrow.

text: In "Mean Girls", who has breasts that tell when it's raining?
explanation: The correct answer is Karen Smith.

text: Which town is the setting for the Disney movie The Love Bug (1968)?
explanation: The correct answer is San Francisco.

text: Which James Bond film had the theme song written and performed by English singer-songwriter Adele?
explanation: The correct answer is Skyfall.

text: Who played Batman in the 1997 film "Batman and Robin"?
explanation: The correct answer is George Clooney.

text: In "Jurassic World", which company purchases InGen and creates Jurassic World?
explanation: The correct answer is Masrani Global Corporation .

text: Brendan Fraser starred in the following movies, except which one?
explanation: The correct answer is Titanic.

text: What is the name of the assassin in the first "Hellboy" movie?
explanation: The correct answer is Karl Ruprecht Kroenen.

text: In what year was the first Indian movie submitted for an Oscar?
explanation: The correct answer is 1957.

text: Which movie did NOT feature the late actor John Candy?
explanation: The correct answer is Ghostbusters.

text: What is the name of the robot in the 1951 science fiction film classic 'The Day the Earth Stood Still'?
explanation: The correct answer is Gort.

text: What did the first moving picture depict?
explanation: The correct answer is A galloping horse.

text: Which actor plays the character "Tommy Jarvis" in "Friday the 13th: The Final Chapter" (1984)?
explanation: The correct answer is Corey Feldman.

text: In the movie “The Iron Giant,” this character is the protagonist.
explanation: The correct answer is Hogarth Hughes.

text: In the "Jurassic Park" universe, what was the first dinosaur cloned by InGen in 1986?
explanation: The correct answer is Velociraptor.

text: The 2016 Disney animated film 'Moana' is based on which culture?
explanation: The correct answer is Polynesian.

text: What was the wifi password given to Stephen Strange in Doctor Strange?
explanation: The correct answer is Shambala.

text: What was the name of the planet in "Aliens"?
explanation: The correct answer is LV-426.

text: Which of these movies did Jeff Bridges not star in?
explanation: The correct answer is The Hateful Eight.

text: In the 2012 film, "The Lorax", who is the antagonist?
explanation: The correct answer is Aloysius O'Hare.

text: What mutated animals act as monsters in the movie 'Night of the Lepus'?
explanation: The correct answer is Rabbits.

text: This movie contains the quote, "Houston, we have a problem."
explanation: The correct answer is Apollo 13.

text: What movie starring Bruce Willis contains the line "I see dead people"?
explanation: The correct answer is The Sixth Sense.

text: This movie contains the quote, "I feel the need ... the need for speed!"
explanation: The correct answer is Top Gun.

text: In the movie "Cast Away" the main protagonist's best friend while on the island is named
explanation: The correct answer is Wilson.

text: In which movie does Robin Williams' character have to disguise themselves into a woman?
explanation: The correct answer is Mrs. Doubtfire.

text: What is the name of the queen's pet in A Bug's Life?
explanation: The correct answer is Aphie.

text: Which one of these films are shot entirely in one-take?
explanation: The correct answer is Russian Ark.

text: Mark Wahlberg played the titular character of which 2008 video-game adaptation?
explanation: The correct answer is Max Payne.

text: What is the name of James Dean's character in the 1955 movie "Rebel Without a Cause"?
explanation: The correct answer is Jim Stark.

text: Which of the following actors does not play a role in the movie "The Usual Suspects?"
explanation: The correct answer is Steve Buscemi.

text: What is the name of the first "Star Wars" film by release order?
explanation: The correct answer is A New Hope.

text: Who voiced Metalbeard in "The Lego Movie"?
explanation: The correct answer is Nick Offerman.

text: In 2012, which movie won every category in the 32nd "Golden Raspberry Awards"?
options: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
explanation: The correct answer is Jack and Jill.

text: Which 1994 film did Roger Ebert famously despise, saying "I hated hated hated hated hated this movie".
explanation: The correct answer is North.

text: What is the name of the character Nicolas Cage plays in the movie "National Treasure?"
explanation: The correct answer is Benjamin Franklin Gates.

text: In the film "Requiem for a Dream", what drug does Jared Leto's character get addicted to?
explanation: The correct answer is Heroin.

text: What was another suggested name for, the 1985 film, Back to the Future?
explanation: The correct answer is Spaceman From Pluto.

text: This movie contains the quote, "What we've got here is a failure to communicate."
options: In the Heat of the Night
explanation: The correct answer is Cool Hand Luke.

text: In the 2014 film "Birdman", what is the primary instrument in the score?
explanation: The correct answer is Drums.

text: What is the name of the dog that played Toto in the 1939 film "The Wizard of Oz"?
explanation: The correct answer is Terry.

text: Who plays the Nemesis in the Resident Evil movies?
explanation: The correct answer is Matthew Taylor.

text: Which famous singer was portrayed by actor Kevin Spacey in the 2004 biographical film "Beyond the Sea"?
explanation: The correct answer is Bobby Darin.

text: In Youtuber Jerma985's 2014 film "Rat Movie: Mystery of the Mayan Treasure," who is the man who tries to steal the Mayan Treasure?
explanation: The correct answer is Dick Dastardly Richards.

text: What character in the Winnie the Pooh films was added by Disney and does not appear in the original books?
explanation: The correct answer is Gopher.

text: Who is the director of the 1991 film "Silence of the Lambs"?
explanation: The correct answer is Jonathan Demme.

text: What were the Chilled Monkey Brains made from during Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom?
explanation: The correct answer is Custard and Raspberry Sauce.

text: What is the name of the supercomputer located in the control room in "Jurassic Park" (1993)?
explanation: The correct answer is Thinking Machines CM-5.

text: In the 2002 film "Kung Pow! Enter the Fist", Master Pain changes his name to what?
explanation: The correct answer is Betty.

text: In what year does Jurassic World open in the "Jurassic Park" universe?
explanation: The correct answer is 2005.

text: What year did the James Cameron film "Titanic" come out in theaters?
explanation: The correct answer is 1997.

text: Bela Lugosi was a Hungarian-American actor best known for his starring role of what 1931 horror film?
options: The Creature from the Black Lagoon
explanation: The correct answer is Count Dracula.

text: What was the first movie to ever use a Wilhelm Scream?
explanation: The correct answer is Distant Drums.

text: Who plays the character of Po in the Kung Fu Panda movies?
explanation: The correct answer is Jack Black.

text: In the Super Mario Bros. Movie (2023), who plays Toad?
explanation: The correct answer is Keegan-Michael Key.

text: Before "Jurassic Park III" was finalized and the Spinosaurus was chosen, which dinosaur was going to be the star?
explanation: The correct answer is Baryonyx.

text: When does "Rogue One: A Star Wars Story" take place chronologically in the series?
explanation: The correct answer is Between Episode 3 and 4.

text: What was Marilyn Monroe`s character's first name in the film "Some Like It Hot"?
explanation: The correct answer is Sugar.

text: What is the title of the first feature-length sound film in history, released in 1927?
explanation: The Warner Bros. film 'The Jazz Singer' contained synchronized speech and singing.

text: Which legendary silent film comedy actor created the famous Little Tramp character?
explanation: Charlie Chaplin first appeared as the Little Tramp character in 1914.

text: Which 1995 Pixar animated film was the first in history to be entirely created with 3D computer graphics?
explanation: Toy Story was a breakthrough in the field of 3D computer animation.

text: Which British film director is known as the 'Master of Suspense' and directed 'Psycho' and 'The Birds'?
explanation: Alfred Hitchcock pioneered the psychological thriller and suspense genres.

text: Who directed and starred in the film 'Citizen Kane' (1941), widely considered a cinematic masterpiece?
explanation: Orson Welles was only 25 years old when he directed 'Citizen Kane'.

text: Which Japanese director made the cult historical films 'Rashomon' and 'Seven Samurai'?
explanation: Akira Kurosawa strongly influenced global and Western cinema.

text: In which 1928 short animated film did Mickey Mouse first appear with synchronized sound?
explanation: 'Steamboat Willie' served as the official screen debut of Mickey Mouse.

text: Which sci-fi film by James Cameron (2009) revolutionized the use of motion capture technology?
explanation: 'Avatar' utilized advanced camera systems and virtual rendering of actors.

text: Which German expressionist director made the legendary dystopian film 'Metropolis' (1927)?
explanation: Fritz Lang directed one of the greatest silent masterpieces of sci-fi.

text: Which artistic movement in cinema of the late 1950s was represented by Francois Truffaut and Jean-Luc Godard?
explanation: The French New Wave brought free-style shooting, handheld cameras, and jump cuts to cinema.

text: What is the name of the famous sound effect (scream) used in more than 400 films since 1951?
explanation: The Wilhelm scream first appeared in the Warner Bros. western 'Distant Drums'.

text: Which singer played the main character in 'The Jazz Singer' (1927) and performed the first vocal track in sound cinema?
explanation: Al Jolson spoke the famous line: 'Wait a minute, wait a minute, you ain't heard nothin' yet!'.
